About the Program
Lead is a toxic metal that can harm the brain, kidneys, and other organs, especially in children. Even a small amount of lead in a child’s body can cause learning difficulties and developmental delays. Lead poisoning can occur when lead enters the body through various sources in the environment. This can be small amounts consumed over time that can lead to health problems.
The Solano County Childhood Lead Poisoning Prevention Program (CLPPP) is a state funded, county administered program established for the prevention, education, screening, diagnosis, and case management of lead poisoning in children and young adults.
The program provides eligible children and young adults with:
case management
environmental surveillance
home visiting
education and outreach for the residents of Solano County.
Eligibility
Solano County children and young adults from birth up to 21 years old with an elevated lead level test result ≥ 3.5 micrograms per deciliter (mcg/dL).
